original_description: "Design It Twice" methodology for APIs, modules, and contracts. Launches parallel sub-agents with opposing constraints to explore radically different designs before committing to one. Use when: designing an API, module interface, data contract, or any public-facing surface. Trigger on: "diseña la interfaz", "cómo debería ser X", "qué API usar", "diseña el contrato", "design an interface", "design the API", "what should the interface look like".
best_description: "Design It Twice" methodology for APIs, modules, and contracts. Launches parallel sub-agents with opposing constraints to explore radically different designs before committing to one. Use when: designing an API, module interface, data contract, or any public-facing surface. Trigger on: "diseña la interfaz", "cómo debería ser X", "qué API usar", "diseña el contrato", "design an interface", "design the API", "what should the interface look like".
final_description: Use when a user is designing — not implementing — an API, interface, module contract, hook, or SDK surface and is undecided about which shape to use. Prime signals: wanting to explore multiple design options before coding, asking which pattern to choose (callbacks vs events, builder vs factory), expressing uncertainty about the interface shape, or explicitly requesting alternatives to compare before committing. Generates three parallel designs with opposing constraints (minimal, flexible, ergonomic) and recommends one. Skip when the interface shape is already decided and the task is implementation, testing, debugging, or code review.

history.description: "Design It Twice" methodology for APIs, modules, and contracts. Launches parallel sub-agents with opposing constraints to explore radically different designs before committing to one. Use when: designing an API, module interface, data contract, or any public-facing surface. Trigger on: "diseña la interfaz", "cómo debería ser X", "qué API usar", "diseña el contrato", "design an interface", "design the API", "what should the interface look like".

history.description: Invoke when the user needs to choose between competing interface designs before writing code. Use for: APIs, SDKs, TypeScript interfaces, repository contracts, hooks, or any public module surface where the shape is still undecided. Key signals: comparing patterns (builder vs factory, callbacks vs promises vs events), wanting multiple options before committing, asking "how should this API look", "I have several ideas but don't know which is better", "explore options with me before I implement". Generates three parallel designs with opposing constraints — minimal surface, maximum flexibility, golden-path ergonomics — then compares and recommends. Skip when: design is already decided, the task is implementing/debugging/testing/reviewing code, or the question is about internal logic rather than public interface shape.
